query: Zinc phosphate cement setting time manipulation methods

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Mechanism of Setting
retrieved_docs.content: The setting of GIC is through an acid-base reaction involving the following stages: Dissolution and decomposition: after mixing the powder and liquid, the acid dissolves the surface of the glass particles to release SiO 4 4- , Ca 2+ , Na + , and F - ions. Initial setting: the Ca 2+ released in the aqueous medium reacts with polyacrylic acid to form a 3-dimensional cross-linked structure. At this time, the material has low strength, and it can be carved using sharp instruments. Final setting: the setting reaction continues during the next 24 hours. During this time, SiO 4 4- forms the silica gel. The slow-moving Al 3+ ions enter the aqueous medium and cross-link the polyacrylic acid chains, displacing the Ca 2+ ions. This increases the final strength of the set cement. Maturation: the aluminum and calcium cross-linked polyacrylate chains get hydrated over time by absorbing water from the aqueous medium. This process is known as maturation. [5] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Manipulation
retrieved_docs.content: Mixing is performed with an agate spatula. A stainless steel spatula is not used because its surface gets abraded by glass particles, contaminating the mixture. The powder and liquid are used in the proportions recommended by the manufacturer (see Image . Mixing Glass Ionomer Cement). The powder is divided into 2 equal increments. The liquid is dispensed later to prevent increased viscosity by water loss on environmental exposure.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: The powder-to-liquid ratio determines the mechanical properties of the set cement. More powder increases the consistency of the cement, strength, and speed of the setting reaction. However, the powder-to-liquid ratio can be increased until a critical point. The volume of the physical matrix will be deficient in binding and holding the cement ingredients together; the mechanical properties will be compromised significantly. Thus, the powder-to-liquid ratio should strictly comply with the manufacturer's instructions. Using pre-graduated self-contained mixing capsules solves this problem. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Impression Materials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: 4. Setting reaction: It sets in 2 steps: first, by the hydrolysis of ZnO to form zinc hydroxide (water is required to initiate the reaction). The second stage is a typical acid-base reaction in which zinc hydroxide reacts with eugenolic acid to form zinc eugenolate, forming a chelate and water as a byproduct. The final set structure consists of ZnO particles embedded in the matrix of zinc eugenolate. The impression paste's setting time can be altered in a clinical setup according to need, either decreased by adding a drop of water during mixing or increased by adding vaseline during mixing. The material has a good flow, which records fine details. ZOE's impression has excellent dimensional stability for 24 hrs; therefore, it can be poured within this time. However, the special impression tray underneath should not deform. [11] 5. Indications:

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Manipulation
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement can also present as a capsule activated by vibrating it in an auto-mixer. The vibration mixes the cement, and the already mixed GIC paste can be used for the chosen clinical indication.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanism of Setting
retrieved_docs.content: Setting occurs by light-induced polymerization. The acid-base setting mechanism does not exist due to the absence of polyacids from the composition.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Dispersed phase glasses: using dispersed phases of strengthening crystallites like aluminum oxide, titanium oxide, and zirconium oxide improved the strength. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement has compressive strength equivalent to zinc phosphate cement, whereas its tensile strength is slightly higher than zinc phosphate cement. Type I GIC has lower compressive strength than Type II GIC. GIC is less stiff and has a lower modulus of elasticity comparable to dentin but lesser than zinc phosphate cement. [8] The resilience of GIC is similar to dentin, which is why it is known as man-made dentin.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Manipulation
retrieved_docs.content: The first increment is added to the liquid and mixed using a folding motion for 15 seconds within a limited area on the mixing pad (see Image . Folding Glass Ionomer Cement). The second increment is added to adjust the consistency. The resultant mixture should have a glossy surface (see Image . Freshly Mixed Glass Ionomer Cement). Such a surface indicates that an adequate number of carboxylic ions are available, crucial for forming chemical bonds with the tooth surface. Mixtures with an opaque surface should be discarded (see Image . Poor Mix Adhesion).

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: The longevity of glass ionomer cement restorations is affected by occlusal forces, porosities in set cement, desiccation or water absorption during initial setting, and use of the mixed cement after the loss of gloss. Furthermore, larger GIC particles decrease the wear rate; the cement's solubility depends on the amount of cement at the margins.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Composition
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement is generally supplied as a powder-liquid system that is mixed manually. The powder is fluoro-aluminosilicate glass, and the liquid is an aqueous solution of polyacrylic acid. Polyacrylic acid is copolymerized with carboxylic acid, maleic, tartaric, and itaconic acid to regulate the viscosity and stabilize the liquid. The liquid exhibits a thixotropic behavior: its thickness can be reversed by shaking or warming the bottle. Other supply options include capsules, twin syringes, and single-bottle water-settable form (freeze-dried polyacrylic acid added to GIC powder). [3] [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Nursing, Allied Health, and Interprofessional Team Interventions
retrieved_docs.content: The chairside dental assistant is in charge of mixing the glass ionomer cement on a glass slab or mixing pad with an agate spatula and handing it to the dentist. The mixing technique is explained in further detail above in the manipulation section. Dental assistants must be well informed on adequately preparing the GIC, instruments needed, and the material setting time.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Cermet ionomer cement: manufactured by sintering the metal and glass powders together, which helps achieve a strong metal bond to the glass. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Materials: Biodentine, a Calcium Silicate Bioactive -- Issues of Concern -- Setting Reaction and Interaction with Dentin
retrieved_docs.content: Biodentine is a 2-part dental material with specific compositions for its powder and liquid components. The powder's primary component is tricalcium silicate. [2] Calcium carbonate and oxide act as fillers. [2] Zirconium oxide is added to the powder for X-ray visibility, and iron oxide is used for shade. [2] The liquid contains calcium chloride, acting as a setting accelerator and a water-reducing agent. [2] Calcium chloride accelerates the setting time of the material and prevents crack formation, a common issue in cement with high water content. The initial setting time for Biodentine is 9-12 minutes. [2] Saliva and blood contamination can delay setting. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Dimensional changes from the sintering process of Y-TZP can lead to non-uniform cement space in single copings. The manufacturing process of Y-TZP blanks may influence the sintering shrinkage pattern and, consequently, on the prostheses fit.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Impression Materials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Zinc oxide eugenol (ZOE) impression material is an irreversible nonelastic material used as a final impression (wash impression) in complete denture fabrication. It records the tissues in the undistorted state. [9] [10] 1. Composition: ZOE impression material is available in 2 collapsible tubes; 2 contain the base and the other the catalyst (see Image . Zinc Oxide Eugenol). The base contains ZnO 87%, vegetable or mineral oils 13%. The catalyst contains eugenol 12-15%, rosin 50%, fillers 20%, resinous balsam 10%, accelerator, and color pigments 5%. *Noneugenol pastes (for patients who are allergic to eugenol) contain carboxylic acids. 2. Instruments and armamentarium: paper pad or glass slab and broad-end spatula. 3. Manipulation: equal lengths are squeezed out from both tubes on a paper pad or a glass slab and mixed with a broad-end spatula until they are mixed to an even color.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 1198
retrieved_docs.content: Nasogastric tube procedures classify the nasogastric tube, indicating that the range of clinical actions, techniques, and protocols are organized around the use, insertion, management, and care of the nasogastric tube.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2927
retrieved_docs.content: Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery are classified as a subset of other procedures to assist delivery, indicating that these specific interventions are encompassed within the broader category of techniques used to facilitate and support the childbirth process.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2394
retrieved_docs.content: Manual conversion of position is a medical procedure that is performed on the uterus.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2327
retrieved_docs.content: Application of therapeutic agent and delivery of vertex presentation are both classified as child procedures within the broader category of surgical manipulation, indicating that these specific interventions are considered subtypes of surgical manipulation in medical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 4 -- Community 2904
retrieved_docs.content: Internal obstetrical version and manual conversion of position are both specific procedures classified as surgical manipulation techniques. Each of these methods is considered a child procedure within the broader category of surgical manipulation, reflecting their role as specialized interventions used to alter fetal position during obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2909
retrieved_docs.content: Delivery by double application of forceps is a obstetric procedure in which the fetal head serves as the direct site of the intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2430
retrieved_docs.content: Other miscellaneous procedures are classified as a subset of other nonoperative procedures, indicating that they fall under the broader category of nonoperative interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2324
retrieved_docs.content: Induction by cervical dilation and other procedures to assist delivery are closely related interventions used to facilitate childbirth, and they may be considered synonymous in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2421
retrieved_docs.content: Antepartum Procedures is a medical category that includes, as its child, the subcategory Antepartum Procedures: Application, Insertion, Removal, indicating that the broader group of antepartum interventions is organized into specific actions of applying, inserting, and removing devices or treatments during the prenatal period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2056
retrieved_docs.content: Surgical Manipulation is a broad category of operative interventions that includes specific techniques such as Application of Therapeutic Agent and Manual Conversion of Position. Application of Therapeutic Agent refers to the intra‑operative delivery of medication or other therapeutic substances, while Manual Conversion of Position involves the physical repositioning of tissues, organs, or skeletal structures during a procedure. Both Application of Therapeutic Agent and Manual Conversion of Position are considered child concepts within Surgical Manipulation, illustrating the diverse range of actions that fall under this overarching surgical domain.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 2106
retrieved_docs.content: Induction and delivery procedures encompass a range of interventions used to initiate and manage labor, with medical induction of labor identified as a specific type of procedure within this broader category.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 4 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: Application of therapeutic agent, Combined obstetrical version, and Manual conversion of position are all specific types of surgical manipulation. As child procedures of the broader category of surgical manipulation, each involves an operative technique aimed at altering a physiological condition during a medical intervention. Application of therapeutic agent refers to the intra‑operative delivery of medication to achieve a therapeutic effect directly at the target site. Combined obstetrical version denotes a coordinated obstetric maneuver performed surgically to reposition a fetus to a more favorable presentation for delivery. Manual conversion of position describes a hands‑on technique used during surgery to adjust the patient’s or organ’s orientation to improve access or outcomes. Recognizing these procedures as subcategories of surgical manipulation helps clinicians select appropriate operative strategies, anticipate procedural requirements, and optimize patient care through targeted therapeutic and positional interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2071
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures comprise a range of interventions used to manage complex obstetric conditions and facilitate safe delivery. Among these, the surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy and abdominal pregnancy represents critical operative approaches for addressing abnormal implantation of the embryo, thereby preventing life‑threatening complications for the mother. In addition, Vaginal Assisted Delivery is included as a procedural option that supports the progression of labor and assists in the birth of the infant when additional support is required. Together, these child procedures under the umbrella of Pregnancy-Related Procedures illustrate the spectrum of surgical and assisted techniques employed in modern obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 997
retrieved_docs.content: positioning methods, and a spectrum of complications—including cervical dystocia, uterine atony, prolonged or stimulated labor, and pelvic‑malformation‑related difficult deliveries—providing a comprehensive framework for understanding labor‑related care and its associated indications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 4 -- Community 2920
retrieved_docs.content: Intrapartum Fetal Stimulation and Manual Fetal Rotation are both classified as إجراءات توليدية (obstetric procedures).
